title: ISW Confirms Russian Net Territory Loss of 116 sq km in April 2026
summary: The Institute for the Study of War assessed that Russian forces lost a net 116 square kilometers of controlled territory in April 2026 — the first net loss since Ukraine's August 2024 Kursk incursion, with advance rates declining to 2.9 sq km per day.
contents:
## Russian Net Territory Loss in April 2026

### ISW Assessment
The Institute for the Study of War (ISW) released its May 2 assessment confirming that Russian forces suffered a net loss of 116 square kilometers of controlled territory in the Ukrainian theater during April 2026. This represented the first net territorial loss since Ukraine's August 2024 incursion into Kursk Oblast.

### Declining Advance Rates
Russian forces seized an average of 2.9 square kilometers per day in the first four months of 2026, compared to 9.76 square kilometers per day during the same period in 2025. Over six months (November 2025 to April 2026), Russian forces captured 1,443.35 square kilometers versus 2,368.38 in the equivalent prior period.

### Contributing Factors
Multiple factors drove the decline:
- Continued Ukrainian ground counterattacks
- Ukrainian mid-range strikes against Russian positions
- February 2026 block on Russia's Starlink terminal usage in Ukraine
- Kremlin's throttling of Telegram communications
- Seasonal rasputitsa (muddy season) effects
- Winter 2025-2026 conditions — approximately three degrees Fahrenheit colder and significantly wetter than the previous winter

### Air Campaign Challenges
Russian milbloggers identified problems with the air campaign, noting that while Su-34 bombers could now carry up to six glide bombs per sortie, insufficient aircraft, maintenance personnel, and poor target prioritization prevented effective exploitation of increased bombing capability.

### Ukrainian Strike Campaign
Ukrainian forces struck Russian oil infrastructure to their lowest refinery output since December 2009, averaging 4.69 million barrels per day. Ukrainian forces also confirmed strikes against Su-57 and Su-34 jets at Shagol Airfield in Chelyabinsk Oblast, 1,676 kilometers from the border.

links.label: ISW - Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment May 2 2026
